stats.title: Statistics
stats.noSessionsHint: Play a game to see your stats here

placeholders.simplePerformanceBody: Overall comparisons are intentionally limited here. Use the Mode filter to display more precise and better-contextualized indicators.
placeholders.simpleEvolutionBody: Trends are easier to read when analyzed mode by mode. Use the Mode filter to display a more relevant evolution.
placeholders.advancedPerformanceBody: The UPS synthesis, distribution and overall breakdown remain reserved for the alpha. For a thorough analysis, use the Mode filter.
placeholders.advancedEvolutionBody: Advanced trends are more reliable when read in the context of a specific mode. Use the Mode filter to display this view.

report.runUnavailable: Correction report unavailable for this run.
report.errorLoadingDetail: The pipeline is blocked or failed.

confidence.behavioralScoreDesc: Behavioral score based on timing, RT consistency, press stability, error awareness (PES), and micro-lapses (focus).
confidence.notIncluded: Not included in the score for this session (x0).

unifiedReport.upsTooltip: Unified score (0-100) comparable across all modes. Combines accuracy and confidence.
unifiedReport.upsNotComputedWithCount: Not computed for this session ({count} responses).
unifiedReport.noDetails: Details not available for this session
unifiedReport.correctDesc: Replay this session to correct your mistakes. New answers replace previous ones.
unifiedReport.replayDesc: Watch this session to review your mistakes. Your results stay unchanged.
unifiedReport.dualnbackClassicThresholdExplanation: Less than 3 errors per modality to validate
unifiedReport.bwThresholdExplanation: 80%+ with discipline = advance • 50-79% = steady progress • Below 50% = time to practice
unifiedReport.bwStrikeExplanation: 3 missed sessions in a row: return to consolidate your skills.
unifiedReport.bwRuleStrike: Below 50% counts as a miss, and after 3 consecutive misses you drop back down.

bwMessages.stay: Stable score. Keep practicing at this level.
bwMessages.down: Insufficient score. You step back to consolidate.
bwMessages.strikeFirst: First session below target. Keep going at this level.
bwMessages.strikeSecond: Two consecutive sessions below target. Stay at this level.
bwMessages.strikeThird: Three consecutive sessions not validated. You return to the previous level.

hybridJourneyMessages.up: The DNB session validates the hybrid pair. You move up to the next level.
hybridJourneyMessages.stay: The DNB session keeps the hybrid pair at this level. The next loop starts again with Track.
hybridJourneyMessages.down: The DNB session drops the hybrid pair by one level. The next loop starts one level lower.
hybridJourneyMessages.pendingPair: This Track session only prepares the decision. Progression will be decided after the DNB session.
hybridJourneyMessages.rule: In this journey, only the DNB session decides up / stay / down.
hybridJourneyMessages.trackBody: Track advances the loop. The next decision will happen during the DNB session.
hybridJourneyMessages.validationBody: This session is going in the right direction. You stay on this level for now; {remaining} more validation will move you up.
hybridJourneyMessages.stayProgressBody: You are in the stay zone. The level remains the same and the DNB loop continues.
hybridJourneyMessages.failureBody: This session counts as a failure. You stay on this level for now, but one more failure will move you down.

unifiedReport.journeyCurrentStateNotice: This report is no longer aligned with the current journey state. Start from the current journey step instead.
unifiedReport.journeyMoreRecentSessionNotice: You have a more recent session in this journey: N-{{level}} ({{date}}).
unifiedReport.rewardLifetimeEarned: You have earned lifetime Premium access

rewards.lifetimeEarned: You have earned lifetime Premium access

tooltips.dPrime: SDT metric: measures ability to discriminate targets. d' of 2.0 or above = good performance.
tooltips.brainWorkshop: Score = (Hits + Correct Rejections − False Alarms − Misses) ÷ Total. False alarms hurt your score!

unifiedReport.trendNotEnoughSessions: Play {{count}} more session(s) to see your progress
unifiedReport.trendSource: Based on your last {{count}} sessions in the same mode

components.timingHelp: Share of responses made during stimulus display (lower = better). Can be disabled (x0) on some sessions.
components.rtConsistencyHelp: Stability of reaction times (consistency). Measured on the first action of each trial (second responses in double-match are not mixed).
components.inputControlHelp: Input quality (touch/mouse): penalizes misfires and accidental double taps.
components.pressStabilityHelp: Stability of press duration (keyboard/controller). On touch/mouse it can be disabled (x0) because it is too noisy.
components.inhibitionHelp: Inhibition: the fewer non-target presses (false alarms), the higher the score.
components.pesHelp: After an error: slowing down (PES) and recovery (accuracy).
components.focusHelp: Micro-lapses (very slow actions) + interruptions (leaving the app / losing focus).

unifiedReport.confidenceNotComputed: Not computed for this session ({count} responses, min {min}).
unifiedReport.bwRuleDown: If 3 consecutive misses: back to the previous level

mode.title: Games
mode.brainWorkshopDesc: Classic scoring: accuracy minus penalties. Based on the open-source Brain Workshop software.
mode.dualPickDesc: Label each stimulus: N, N-1, N-2.
mode.dualTrackDesc: Track multiple moving targets, then identify them.
mode.runningSpanDesc: Recall last N items from unpredictable stream

journey.title: Journey
journey.simBrainworkshopDesc: Classic scoring journey with penalties for false alarms
journey.dualTrackDnbHybridDesc: Hybrid journey alternating Dual Track then Dual N-Back Classic

history.title: Session history
history.noSessionsHint: Play a game to see your results here

delete.sessionWarning: This will delete the session and all its corrections.
delete.sessionInfo: The session from {{date}} (N-{{level}}) will be permanently deleted.
delete.bulkWarning: This action is irreversible. All selected sessions will be permanently deleted.

messages.streakSubtitle: Your consecutive days streak shows real discipline. Keep it up!
messages.excellentSubtitle: You're mastering Dual N-Back. Keep pushing your limits!
messages.goodSubtitle: You're progressing well. A few more sessions to reach excellence.
messages.progressingSubtitle: Your efforts are paying off. Keep up this pace!
messages.keepGoingSubtitle: Regular training is key. Every session strengthens your memory.
messages.startingSubtitle: Welcome! A few sessions are enough to see significant progress.

dualnbackClassic.errorRateFormula: Errors = omissions + false alarms across all responses

advanced.intro: In-depth analysis of your performance with psychometric metrics.
advanced.cvDuringOnlyNote: CV is computed primarily on during-window responses; otherwise on all responses (preferably onset-based).
advanced.pesNoErrorsDesc: No errors to analyze for this metric
advanced.rtDefinitionNote: Note: during/after is inferred from game state; RT may mix onset/offset.
advanced.phaseMachineNote: During/after is inferred from game state (stimulus vs waiting).

progression.title: Progression
progression.noProgressionHint: Play a game to start your progression

tooltips.dprimeDesc: Measures your ability to distinguish true matches from false alarms. Higher is better.
tooltips.passRateDesc: Percentage of sessions where you reached the success threshold (d-prime ≥ 1.5). Indicates your consistency.
tooltips.maxLevelDesc: The highest N-Back level you've reached. Your personal record!
tooltips.totalTimeDesc: Total time spent training. Consistency is key to progress.
tooltips.trendDesc: Evolution of your performance over recent sessions. Calculated by comparing your recent average to your overall average.
tooltips.confidenceDesc: Reliability of the trend analysis. The more sessions you have, the more accurate the analysis.
tooltips.visualDesc: Spatial memory: remembering the position of squares on the grid
tooltips.audioDesc: Auditory memory: remembering the spoken letters
tooltips.balanceDesc: Compares your performance across both channels. An imbalance reveals your dominant modality.
tooltips.reactionTimeDesc: Average time between stimulus appearance and your response. Lower is faster.
tooltips.rtByModalityDesc: Compares your reaction times across each channel (visual vs auditory). Reveals your fastest modality.
tooltips.rtVariabilityDesc: Coefficient of Variation (CV) = standard deviation / mean. Measures the consistency of your response times. A low CV indicates stable concentration, a high CV suggests attention fluctuations.
tooltips.errorAwarenessDesc: Post-Error Slowing: ratio between response time after an error and average response time on hits. A ratio > 1 means you slow down after an error, indicating good metacognitive awareness.
tooltips.accuracyDesc: Overall percentage of correct responses (hits) across all your sessions
tooltips.streakDesc: Number of consecutive days you've played at least one session. Consistency strengthens memory!
tooltips.sessionsDesc: Total number of completed training sessions
tooltips.currentLevelDesc: Your current N-Back level. N-2 = remember 2 turns back, N-3 = 3 turns, etc.
tooltips.bestHourDesc: The time of day when you typically get the best results. Useful for planning your sessions.
tooltips.nLevelDesc: Number of turns back to remember. N-2 = remember info from 2 turns ago. Higher N levels increase the difficulty.
tooltips.modalitiesDesc: The sensory channels active during training. More channels = more cognitive demand.
tooltips.progressionMLDesc: Uses your history to predict your optimal level. Needs 10+ sessions to be accurate.
tooltips.generatorRandomDesc: Purely random generation. May produce easy or very difficult sequences.
tooltips.generatorBWDesc: Controls % of targets and lures. More balanced and predictable sequences.
tooltips.generatorJaeggiDesc: Faithful reproduction of the Dual N-Back classic protocol (2008). 20 trials with fixed structure.
tooltips.targetsDesc: Percentage of trials where current stimulus matches the one from N turns ago. These are the "matches" to detect.
tooltips.luresDesc: Similar but wrong stimuli (e.g., N-1 or N+1 instead of N). Makes the game harder by creating confusion.
tooltips.stimulusDurationDesc: How long the stimulus is displayed. Too short = hard to see, too long = boring.
tooltips.percentilesDesc: Shows how your sessions are distributed. P25 = 25% are below this, P50 = median, P75 = 75% are below this.
tooltips.upsStdDevDesc: Measures how consistent your UPS scores are. Lower σ = more stable performance. Higher σ = more variable.
tooltips.errorRateDesc: Percentage of wrong actions (missed matches + false alarms) out of all actions
tooltips.omissionsDesc: Missed matches: when there was a match but you did not respond. Often indicates inattention or uncertainty.
tooltips.falseAlarmsDesc: False alarms: when you responded but there was no match. Often indicates impulsivity or confusion.
tooltips.hitRateDesc: Proportion of correctly detected matches. Hits / (Hits + Misses). Higher is better.
tooltips.faRateDesc: Proportion of false alarms among non-matches. FA / (FA + Correct Rejections). Lower is better.
tooltips.activeDaysDesc: Number of distinct days you trained during this period. Regular practice improves results.
tooltips.avgSessionDesc: Average training time per active day in the filtered period
tooltips.maxNDesc: Highest N-Back level reached during this period. Your difficulty ceiling.
tooltips.performanceDesc: Overall accuracy: percentage of correct actions (hits + correct rejections) out of all actions
tooltips.upsDesc: Unified Performance Score (0-100) combining accuracy (60%) and confidence (40%). Comparable across all modes.
tooltips.sessionsPerDayDesc: Number of sessions completed each day. Shows your training frequency.
tooltips.accuracyTrendDesc: How your accuracy evolves over time. Each point shows the daily average/min/max (toggle below).
tooltips.errorRateTrendDesc: Global session error-rate trend (Dual N-Back Classic). Each point shows the daily average/min/max (toggle below). Lower is better.
tooltips.nLevelTrendDesc: How your N-level evolves over time. Each point shows the daily average/min/max level (toggle below).
tooltips.upsEvolutionDesc: How your UPS score evolves over time. Each point shows the daily average/min/max (toggle below). Combines accuracy and confidence into a single score.
tooltips.byModalityDesc: Breakdown of your performance by sensory channel (position, audio, color)
tooltips.byModalityDualnbackClassicDesc: Error rate by modality (classic Dual N-Back 2008). Your worst modality determines your progression.
tooltips.modeScoreDesc: Score specific to this game mode. Calculated using the native scoring method.
tooltips.dualnbackClassicScoreDesc: Global session error rate (Dual N-Back Classic). Lower is better.
tooltips.medianRTDesc: Median response time: 50% of responses are faster, 50% slower. Less affected by outliers than average.
tooltips.focusDesc: Times you switched away from the app during a session. May affect performance.
tooltips.modeBreakdownDesc: Comparison of your stats across different game modes
tooltips.flowConfidenceDesc: Measures the quality of your drag-and-drop accuracy beyond simple correctness. Based on trajectory directness and hesitation time.
tooltips.directnessDesc: Ratio of direct path vs actual path traveled. 100% means a perfectly straight drag to the target.
tooltips.hesitationDesc: Total time spent hovering over wrong slots before dropping. Less hesitation indicates higher confidence.
tooltips.recallConfidenceDesc: Measures the quality of your recall beyond simple accuracy. Based on selection speed and number of corrections.
tooltips.fluencyDesc: How smoothly you made your selections. Based on timing consistency and correction rate.
tooltips.correctionsDesc: Number of times you changed your selection before confirming. Fewer corrections indicate higher confidence.

sublines.impulsiveWithRTV2_one: {{fa}} false alarms with avg RT of {{rt}}ms
sublines.impulsiveWithRTV2_other: {{fa}} false alarms with avg RT of {{rt}}ms
sublines.impulsiveWithRTV3: Tendency to respond too fast: {{fa}} FA, ~{{rt}} ms.
sublines.impulsiveV1: {{fa}} false alarms: take a second to verify.
sublines.balancedV3: Stable profile: no modality is dropping off.
sublines.modalityWeaknessV2: {{modality}} is the limiting factor ({{hits}}/{{total}}): cues are less stable on this channel.
sublines.modalityWeaknessV3: This session, {{modality}} dropped off ({{hits}}/{{total}}): that's the most direct gain.
sublines.correctAndErrorsV1: Summary: {{correct}} correct answers for {{errors}} errors. To improve: identify whether errors are mostly omissions or non-target responses.
sublines.correctAndErrorsV2: {{correct}} correct, {{errors}} errors: the margin comes down to a few decisions. To improve: target the dominant error type.
sublines.correctAndErrorsV3: {{correct}} / {{errors}} (hits / errors): quality depends mostly on the decision. To improve: aim for accuracy.
sublines.missedTargetsV1: Omissions: {{misses}}/{{total}}. The main cost is sustained attention.
sublines.missedTargetsV2: {{misses}} targets missed out of {{total}}: more of a dropout than an over-response.
sublines.missedTargetsV3: Detection at {{pct}}%: targets are slipping under the radar.
sublines.missedTargetsV4: {{detected}}/{{total}} detected: when you respond, it's often correct; the gap is upstream.
sublines.missedTargetsWithPctV1: Omissions {{pct}}% ({{misses}}/{{total}}): tendency to let targets pass. To improve: look for matches first.
sublines.missedTargetsWithPctV2: Miss rate {{pct}}%: the challenge is detection, not speed.
sublines.missedTargetsWithPctV3: {{pct}}% omissions ({{misses}}/{{total}}): information slips past you more than it fools you. To improve: secure detection.
sublines.falseAlarmsV1: {{fa}} false alarms: signal is often weak. To improve: only confirm when the match is certain.
sublines.falseAlarmsV2: Responses on non-targets: {{fa}}. May indicate an overly permissive match criterion. To improve: require a clear cue.
sublines.falseAlarmsV3: {{fa}} responses on non-targets: vigilance is quite aggressive. To improve: slow down slightly before confirming.
sublines.falseAlarmsDetailedV1: False alarms: {{fa}}/{{nonTargets}}. Clear sign of anticipation.
sublines.falseAlarmsDetailedV2: Non-targets flagged: {{fa}}/{{nonTargets}}. Match criterion is too permissive.
sublines.falseAlarmsDetailedV3: False alarm rate: {{pct}}% (response threshold needs raising).
sublines.modalityScoreV2: {{modality}}: {{pct}}% (channel to stabilize first). To improve: slow down slightly on {{modality}}.
sublines.modalityScoreV3: {{modality}}: {{hits}}/{{total}}. Once this channel is dialed in, the rest follows.
sublines.errorsTotalV1: {{errors}} errors in {{trials}} trials: most useful alongside the error signature (omission vs non-target).
sublines.errorsTotalV2: {{success}}/{{trials}} successful: the gap hinges on just a few trials.
sublines.errorsTotalV3: Error rate {{pct}}%: interpret it alongside the dominant error type.
sublines.consolidateV1: Master N-{{level}} before moving up: stabilize your profile.
sublines.consolidateV2: More practice needed at N-{{level}}: aim for consistency.
sublines.modalityComparisonV2: Clear gap: {{best}} ({{bestScore}}) vs {{worst}} ({{worstScore}}). {{worst}} is dragging the session down. To improve: prioritize {{worst}}.
sublines.detectionRateV1: Detection: {{hits}}/{{total}}. The question is: what causes you to miss the rest?
sublines.detectionRateV2: Detection rate {{pct}}%: useful for distinguishing omissions vs false alarms.
sublines.detectionRateV3: {{hits}}/{{total}} targets spotted: when it drops, it's not random.
sublines.stableRTV1: Stable RTs (~{{rt}} ms): the bottleneck is likely decisional. To improve: clarify the match criterion.
sublines.stableRTV2: Steady RTs (~{{rt}} ms): tempo is under control. To improve: focus on detection, not more speed.
sublines.stableRTV3: Temporal consistency (~{{rt}} ms): you're well calibrated. To improve: optimize the quality of your decisions.
sublines.variableRTV1: Variable RTs: likely micro-hesitations. To improve: keep a steady rhythm and confirm later.
sublines.variableRTV2: Irregular rhythm: unstable strategy for the session. To improve: pick a tempo and stick to it.
sublines.variableRTV3: Notable temporal variability: some situations make you hesitate. To improve: reduce corrections.
sublines.focusLostV1: {{count}} focus loss(es) ({{duration}} away from app): may blur detection. To improve: avoid interruptions.
sublines.focusLostV1_one: Focus lost {{count}} time ({{duration}}): may explain misses. Tip: avoid interruptions.
sublines.focusLostV1_other: Focus lost {{count}} times ({{duration}}): may explain misses. Tip: avoid interruptions.
sublines.analystFocusLost_one: {{count}} interruption ({{duration}} away): may disrupt detection. Tip: avoid breaks.
sublines.analystFocusLost_other: {{count}} interruptions ({{duration}} away): may disrupt detection. Tip: avoid breaks.
sublines.analystModalityGapStrong: {{best}} ({{bestScore}}) vs {{worst}} ({{worstScore}}): {{worst}} is the bottleneck. Tip: prioritize {{worst}}.
sublines.analystMissStrong: {{pct}}% omissions ({{misses}}/{{total}}): detection is the key, not speed. Tip: secure obvious matches first.
sublines.analystMissWeak: {{misses}}/{{total}} targets missed (small sample): may indicate a detection gap. Tip: secure obvious matches first.
sublines.analystFaStrong: {{pct}}% responses on non-targets ({{fa}}/{{nonTargets}}): criterion too permissive. Tip: require a clear signal before responding.
sublines.analystFaWeak_one: {{fa}} response on non-target (small sample): may indicate a permissive criterion. Tip: require a clear signal.
sublines.analystFaWeak_other: {{fa}} responses on non-targets (small sample): may indicate a permissive criterion. Tip: require a clear signal.
sublines.analystModalityGapSoft: {{worst}}: {{hits}}/{{total}} (moderate gap). Tip: consolidate {{worst}} in the next sessions.
sublines.analystVariableRt: Variable response timing on this session. Tip: adopt a steady rhythm and respond slightly later.
sublines.analystStableRt: Stable RT (~{{rt}} ms): your pace is set. Tip: aim for decision quality, not more speed.
sublines.analystLevelUp: Session improving, level mastered: try N-{{level}}. Tip: go for the next step.
sublines.analystConsolidate: N-{{level}} to consolidate: stabilize your error profile before moving up. Tip: aim for consistency.
sublines.analystFallback: {{correct}} correct responses, {{errors}} errors. Tip: identify whether the pattern is omissions or non-targets.
sublines.analystFocusLost: {{count}} interruption(s) ({{duration}} away): the rhythm can suffer. Try to avoid breaks.
sublines.analystFocusLostV1: {{count}} interruption(s) ({{duration}} away): may disrupt detection. Try to avoid breaks.
sublines.analystFocusLostV2: {{count}} interruption(s) during the session: detection can drop.
sublines.analystDieselV1: Slow start, strong finish: you warmed up.
sublines.analystDieselV2: You warmed up: the end of the session was cleaner than the start.
sublines.analystDieselV3: Cautious start, solid finish: good sign.
sublines.analystSprinterV1: Strong finish: your accuracy improved late.
sublines.analystSprinterV2: Acceleration at the end: you finished strong.
sublines.analystPesVigilantV1: After errors you slow down ({{ratio}}%): good self-checking.
sublines.analystPesVigilantV2: After an error you take a moment to verify: good vigilance.
sublines.analystPesImpulsiveV1: After an error, you don't slow down ({{ratio}}%): try a brief micro-pause.
sublines.analystPesImpulsiveV2: After an error, add a brief pause before responding.
sublines.analystSymmetricalV1: Clear balance across modalities: very even profile.
sublines.analystSymmetricalV2: Modalities are well aligned: no channel is lagging.
sublines.analystFatigueDetectedV2: Small drop near the end: try to keep the pace steady.
sublines.analystStabilityExpertV1: Very stable timing (~{{rt}} ms): automation is kicking in.
sublines.analystStabilityExpertV2: Very steady timing: you're well calibrated.
sublines.analystModalityGapStrongV2: {{worst}} weighs the most: prioritize it next.
sublines.analystMissStrongV2: Miss rate {{pct}}%: look for obvious matches first.
sublines.analystMissWeakV1: {{misses}}/{{total}} targets missed (small sample): may indicate a detection gap.
sublines.analystMissWeakV2: A few targets were missed: secure the obvious matches first.
sublines.analystFaStrongV1: {{pct}}% responses on non-targets ({{fa}}/{{nonTargets}}): criterion too permissive.
sublines.analystFaStrongV2: Non-target responses at {{pct}}%: wait for a clear signal before confirming.
sublines.analystFaWeak: {{fa}} response(s) on non-targets (small sample): criterion may be slightly permissive.
sublines.analystFaWeakV1: {{fa}} response(s) on non-targets (small sample): criterion may be slightly permissive.
sublines.analystFaWeakV2: A few non-target responses: try being stricter about the match.
sublines.analystModalityGapSoftV2: Moderate gap across modalities: consolidate the weakest channel.
sublines.analystVariableRtV1: Variable rhythm this session: pick a tempo and stick to it.
sublines.analystVariableRtV2: Timing is irregular: respond a bit later, but more consistently.
sublines.analystStableRtV1: Stable RT (~{{rt}} ms): pace is set. Focus on decision quality.
sublines.analystStableRtV2: Stable rhythm: most gains come from cleaner decisions.
sublines.analystLevelUpV1: Improving session: try N-{{level}} next time.
sublines.analystLevelUpV2: You can try stepping up on the next session.
sublines.analystConsolidateV1: Consolidate N-{{level}}: aim for consistency before moving up.
sublines.analystConsolidateV2: Consolidate first: the next level comes with steadier accuracy.
sublines.analystFallbackV1: {{correct}} correct, {{errors}} errors: identify the dominant error type.
sublines.analystFallbackV2: Overall: identify whether the pattern is more omissions or non-target responses.
sublines.simpleBalancedExpertV2: Modalities are pretty even this session.
sublines.simpleBalancedExpertV3: Stable profile: no modality is lagging.
sublines.simpleSuggestStabilityV1: Irregular rhythm: try a steadier tempo.
sublines.simpleSuggestStabilityV2: Next time, keep your rhythm more consistent.
sublines.simpleMissFocusV1: {{misses}} omissions: focus on spotting targets first.
sublines.simpleMissFocusV2: Some targets were missed: secure the obvious matches first.
sublines.simpleFaPatientV1: {{fa}} responses on non-targets: slow down before confirming.
sublines.simpleFaPatientV2: A few false alarms: only confirm if the match is clear.

modeScore.dprimeTooltip: Signal Detection Theory: d' of 2.0 or above = good discrimination
modeScore.jaeggiErrorsTooltip: Error percentage (misses + false alarms). Fewer than 3 errors per modality to progress.

corsi.noDetailedTimeline: Detailed trial history is unavailable for this session.

dualTrack.calibrationDescription: This session calibrated your starting level before the first journey stage.
dualTrack.currentStage: Current target-count level in the journey
dualTrack.journeyProgressBody: This session counts toward your Dual Track journey progression.
dualTrack.journeyRegressionBody: The journey steps down one tier to consolidate your tracking.
dualTrack.journeyPromotedBody: The next journey session moves to the next target count.
dualTrack.tiersRemaining: Tiers remaining before the next target-count level
dualTrack.tierHelp: T = internal journey difficulty tier. The higher the number, the more demanding tracking becomes at this target level.
dualTrack.tierHelpCurrent: T = internal difficulty tier. You are currently at T{{current}} out of {{total}}. There are {{remaining}} tier(s) left before the next target level.
dualTrack.tierHelpNext: The next visible tier will be T{{current}} out of {{total}}. After that, there will be {{remaining}} tier(s) left before the next target level.
dualTrack.tierHelpReset: The next target level has just been unlocked. You restart at T{{current}} out of {{total}}, with {{remaining}} tier(s) before the following target level.
dualTrack.stageUnlocked: New target stage unlocked for the next session.

stopSignal.ssrtSlow: Slower inhibition profile. Focus on clean stop-trial control.

ospan.noDetailedSets: Detailed set history is unavailable for this session.
